501.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ubaydah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'A person in a state of major ritual impurity (janabah) may pass through the mosque but must not sit in it.' He then recited the verse: '...nor while in a state of major impurity, unless passing through.' (Quran 4:43)
501.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ubaydah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Orang yang junub boleh melewati masjid, tetapi tidak boleh duduk di dalamnya.' Kemudian ia membaca ayat: '...dan tidak pula junub, kecuali sekadar melewati jalan.' (QS. An-Nisa: 43)

502.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ikrimah and Sa'id that they both said: 'A person in a state of major ritual impurity may pass through the mosque but must not sit in it.'
502.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ikrimah dan Sa'id bahwa keduanya berkata: 'Orang yang junub boleh melewati masjid tetapi tidak boleh duduk di dalamnya.'

503.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We used to walk through the mosque while in a state of major ritual impurity, and we saw no harm in that.'
503.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami biasa berjalan melalui masjid dalam keadaan junub, dan kami tidak melihat mengapa itu bermasalah.'

504.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ata' regarding a menstruating woman who was wearing an amulet or written charm around her neck: 'If it is in leather, she should remove it. If it is in a silver tube, there is no harm — she may keep it on or remove it as she wishes.'
504.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Atha' mengenai wanita haid yang mengenakan jimat atau tulisan di lehernya: 'Jika berada dalam kulit, hendaklah ia melepasnya. Jika berada dalam tabung perak, tidak apa-apa — ia boleh memakainya atau melepasnya sesuai keinginannya.'

505.iwh_en: It was narrated from Matar that he said: 'I asked al-Hasan and Ata' about a man traveling with his wife who menstruates and then becomes pure but cannot find water. They both said: She performs tayammum and her husband may be intimate with her.'
505.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mathar bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada al-Hasan dan Atha' tentang seorang pria yang bepergian bersama istrinya yang haid kemudian suci namun tidak menemukan air. Keduanya berkata: Ia bertayammum dan suaminya boleh menggaulinya.'

506.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ata' that he said regarding a woman who becomes pure but cannot find water: 'Her husband may be intimate with her after she performs tayammum.' When Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak was asked whether he held this opinion, he said: 'Yes, by Allah.'
506.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Atha' bahwa ia berkata tentang wanita yang suci dari haid namun tidak menemukan air: 'Suaminya boleh menggaulinya setelah ia bertayammum.' Ketika Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak ditanya apakah ia berpendapat demikian, ia berkata: 'Ya, demi Allah.'

507.iwh_en: It was narrated from Tawus regarding the waiting period (istibra') for a slavegirl who does not menstruate: 'Forty-five days.'
507.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Thawus mengenai masa tunggu (istibra') bagi budak perempuan yang tidak haid: 'Empat puluh lima hari.'

508.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qilabah that he said regarding the waiting period for a slavegirl who does not menstruate: 'Three months.'
508.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qilabah bahwa ia berkata mengenai masa tunggu bagi budak perempuan yang tidak haid: 'Tiga bulan.'

509.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Awzai that he said: 'I asked al-Zuhri about a man who purchases a slavegirl who has not yet reached the age of menstruation nor is she the kind whose pregnancy is possible — how long must he wait before being intimate with her? He said: Three months.' Yahya ibn Abi Kathir also said: 'Three months.'
509.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Awza'i bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada al-Zuhri tentang seorang pria yang membeli budak perempuan yang belum mencapai usia haid dan tidak mungkin hamil — berapa lama ia harus menunggu sebelum menggaulinya? Ia berkata: Tiga bulan.' Yahya ibn Abi Katsir juga berkata: 'Tiga bulan.'

510.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ikrimah that he said regarding the waiting period for a slavegirl who does not menstruate: 'One month.' When Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak was asked which opinion he held, he said: 'Three months is more reliable, though one month is sufficient.'
510.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ikrimah bahwa ia berkata mengenai masa tunggu bagi budak perempuan yang tidak haid: 'Satu bulan.' Ketika Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak ditanya pendapat mana yang ia pegang, ia berkata: 'Tiga bulan lebih kuat, meskipun satu bulan sudah cukup.'

2758.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, will a man be held accountable for what he did during the pre-Islamic period of ignorance?' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever does good deeds in Islam will not be held accountable for what he did in ignorance, but whoever does evil in Islam will be held accountable for the first and the last.'
2758.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang pria berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, apakah seseorang akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas apa yang dilakukannya pada masa Jahiliyah?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berbuat baik dalam Islam tidak akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas perbuatannya di masa Jahiliyah, namun barangsiapa berbuat buruk dalam Islam akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas yang pertama dan yang terakhir.'

2759.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Wadin that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we were a people of ignorance who used to worship idols and kill our children. I have some children whom I fathered during the period of ignorance who died before Islam — what is their fate?' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'They are in the Fire.' When the man turned to leave with a troubled look on his face, the Prophet ﷺ called him back and said: 'My children and your children are together.'
2759.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Wadin bahwa seorang pria datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, kami adalah kaum Jahiliyah yang menyembah berhala dan membunuh anak-anak kami. Aku memiliki beberapa anak yang lahir di masa Jahiliyah dan meninggal sebelum Islam — apa nasib mereka?' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Mereka ada di dalam neraka.' Ketika pria itu berbalik pergi dengan wajah sedih, Nabi ﷺ memanggilnya kembali dan bersabda: 'Anakku dan anakmu bersama-sama.'

2760.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mujahid that his patron said: 'My family sent with me a bowl containing butter and milk for their idols. I was prevented from eating the butter out of fear of the idols. Then the idol fell into the bowl. We looked at it and noticed that a dog had eaten it. From that moment my heart was freed from the idols.'
2760.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mujahid bahwa tuannya berkata: 'Keluargaku mengutus bersamaku sebuah mangkuk berisi mentega dan susu untuk berhala mereka. Aku dicegah memakan mentega itu karena takut kepada berhala. Kemudian berhala itu jatuh ke dalam mangkuk. Kami melihatnya dan menyadari bahwa seekor anjing telah memakannya. Sejak saat itu hatiku terbebas dari berhala.'

2761.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Raja' that he said: 'In the pre-Islamic era, whenever we found a beautiful stone we would worship it. If we could not find a stone, we would gather a mound of sand, then bring a sheep and milk it over the mound and circumambulate it. When the month of Rajab came, we would say: the month for removing iron-heads — and we would strip all arrows of their iron tips and not fight during the whole of Rajab and Sha'ban.'
2761.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Raja' bahwa ia berkata: 'Pada masa Jahiliyah, kapanpun kami menemukan batu yang indah kami menyembahnya. Jika kami tidak dapat menemukan batu, kami mengumpulkan gundukan pasir, lalu membawa seekor domba dan memerahnya di atas gundukan dan mengelilinginya. Ketika bulan Rajab tiba, kami berkata: bulan pencabutan ujung besi — dan kami mencabut semua ujung besi dari panah dan tidak berperang selama Rajab dan Sya'ban.'

2762.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b al-Ahbar (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We find it written: Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah — not harsh or coarse, not clamoring in the marketplaces, does not repay evil with evil but rather pardons and forgives.'
2762.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b al-Ahbar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami mendapatkannya tertulis: Muhammad adalah Rasulullah — tidak kasar dan tidak keras, tidak berteriak-teriak di pasar, tidak membalas kejahatan dengan kejahatan tetapi justru memaafkan dan mengampuni.'

2763.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Salam (may Allah be pleased with him) that he used to say: 'We find the description of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the Torah, and we find it written next to his description: Jesus son of Mary will be born after him, and Jesus's community will not cease to exist until the Hour comes.'
2763.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Salam (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia biasa berkata: 'Kami mendapatkan sifat Rasulullah ﷺ dalam Taurat, dan kami mendapati tertulis di samping sifatnya: Isa putra Maryam akan lahir setelahnya, dan umat Isa tidak akan berhenti ada sampai Kiamat tiba.'

2764.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'In the first line we find: Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, My chosen servant — not harsh or coarse, not clamoring in the marketplaces, does not repay evil with evil but rather pardons and forgives. His birthplace is Makkah, his migration is to Taybah, and his kingdom is in Syria.'
2764.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Pada baris pertama kami menemukan: Muhammad adalah Rasulullah, hamba-Ku yang terpilih — tidak kasar dan tidak keras, tidak berteriak-teriak di pasar, tidak membalas kejahatan dengan kejahatan tetapi justru memaafkan dan mengampuni. Tempat kelahirannya adalah Makkah, hijrahnya ke Taybah, dan kerajaannya ada di Syam.'

2765.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he asked Ka'b al-Ahbar: 'How do you find the description of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the Torah?' Ka'b said: 'We find him described as Muhammad ibn Abdullah, born in Makkah, migrating to Taybah, his kingdom in Syria — not harsh or coarse, not clamoring in the marketplaces. I bear witness that he is the Messenger of Allah and that we find his description in the Torah.'
2765.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia bertanya kepada Ka'b al-Ahbar: 'Bagaimana kamu mendapatkan sifat Rasulullah ﷺ dalam Taurat?' Ka'b berkata: 'Kami mendapatinya digambarkan sebagai Muhammad ibn Abdullah, lahir di Makkah, berhijrah ke Taybah, kerajaannya di Syam — tidak kasar dan tidak keras, tidak berteriak-teriak di pasar. Aku bersaksi bahwa ia adalah Rasulullah dan bahwa kami mendapati sifatnya dalam Taurat.'

2766.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jubayr ibn Nufayr al-Hadrami that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A messenger has come to you from among yourselves — the verse: "There has come to you a Messenger from among yourselves" (Quran 9:128) — grievous to him is your suffering, devoted to you, and to the believers kind and merciful. If they turn away, say: Allah is sufficient for me — there is no deity except Him, in Him I put my trust and He is the Lord of the great Throne.' (Quran 9:129)
2766.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jubayr ibn Nufayr al-Hadrami bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Telah datang kepada kalian seorang rasul dari kaum kalian sendiri — ayat: "Sungguh telah datang kepada kalian seorang rasul dari kaum kalian sendiri" (QS. At-Tawbah: 128) — berat terasa baginya penderitaan kalian, sangat menginginkan keselamatan bagi kalian, dan terhadap orang-orang beriman ia sangat penyantun lagi penyayang. Jika mereka berpaling, katakanlah: Cukuplah Allah bagiku — tidak ada ilah selain Dia, kepada-Nya aku bertawakal dan Dia adalah Tuhan Arsy yang agung.' (QS. At-Tawbah: 129)

2767.iwh_en: It was narrated from Amir that he said: 'A man from among the companions of the Prophet ﷺ needed something from him. The Prophet ﷺ walked with him until he entered his home. The man said: I saw the Prophet ﷺ walking humbly, turning his whole body when he looked, and when he sat, he sat with his knees close together and his thighs joined, as a humble servant sits before his master.'
2767.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Amir bahwa ia berkata: 'Seorang sahabat Nabi ﷺ membutuhkan sesuatu darinya. Nabi ﷺ berjalan bersamanya hingga masuk ke rumahnya. Pria itu berkata: Aku melihat Nabi ﷺ berjalan dengan rendah hati, berbalik seluruh tubuhnya ketika melihat, dan ketika duduk, ia duduk dengan lutut rapat dan paha berdekatan, sebagaimana seorang hamba yang rendah hati duduk di hadapan tuannya.'

2768.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rabi'ah al-Jurashi that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ was brought and it was said to him: Choose whatever you wish from what your Lord has endowed you with.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'I choose gentleness.' It was said to him again. He said: 'I choose gentleness.' Then Gabriel — or another angel — placed his hand between his shoulder blades until he felt its coolness in his chest. After that, he was never afraid of anyone and nothing made him afraid.',
2768.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rabi'ah al-Jurashi bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ didatangi dan dikatakan kepadanya: Pilihlah apa yang kamu inginkan dari apa yang Tuhanmu telah menganugerahkan kepadamu.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku memilih kelembutan.' Dikatakan kepadanya lagi. Ia bersabda: 'Aku memilih kelembutan.' Kemudian Jibril — atau malaikat lain — meletakkan tangannya di antara kedua bahunya hingga ia merasakan kesejukannya di dadanya. Setelah itu, ia tidak pernah takut kepada siapapun dan tidak ada yang membuatnya takut.'

2769.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Uthman al-Nahdi that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out to al-Batha' with Ibn Mas'ud. He had him sit down and drew a line around him, then said: 'Do not go beyond this line, for if you do, you will be harmed.' The Prophet ﷺ then went off and returned, finding Ibn Mas'ud sitting in his place, and said: 'Have you seen anything?' He said: 'Some riders passed by who were pitch black with white garments.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Those were the jinn of Nasibin who came requesting provisions from me, and I provisioned them with bones and dung.'
2769.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Utsman al-Nahdi bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ pergi ke al-Batha' bersama Ibnu Mas'ud. Beliau memintanya duduk dan membuat garis di sekelilingnya, lalu bersabda: 'Jangan melampaui garis ini, karena jika kamu melakukannya, kamu akan mendapat bahaya.' Nabi ﷺ kemudian pergi dan kembali, mendapati Ibnu Mas'ud duduk di tempatnya, dan bersabda: 'Apakah kamu melihat sesuatu?' Ia berkata: 'Beberapa penunggang lewat yang gelap pekat dengan pakaian putih.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Mereka adalah jin dari Nasibin yang datang meminta perbekalan dariku, dan aku memberi mereka tulang dan kotoran.'

2770.iwh_en: It was narrated from Utbah ibn Abd al-Sulami (may Allah be pleased with him), who was a companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: A man will be born among you who will conquer with his right hand more of the earth than Alexander the Great conquered — meaning Jesus son of Mary.'
2770.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Utbah ibn Abd al-Sulami (radhiyallahu 'anhu), yang merupakan sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ, bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Akan lahir di antara kalian seseorang yang akan menaklukkan dengan tangan kanannya lebih banyak bumi daripada yang ditaklukkan oleh Iskandar Agung — yakni Isa putra Maryam.'

2771.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I said: O Messenger of Allah, how did you know that you were a prophet until you were certain of it?' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Abu Dharr, two angels came to me while I was in a valley of Makkah. One of them descended to earth and the other remained in the sky. It was said: Is this him? It was said: Yes. It was said: Weigh him against a man. I outweighed him. Then it was said: Weigh him against a hundred men. I outweighed them. Then it was said: Weigh him against a thousand men. I outweighed them. So the one in the sky said to the one on earth: Even if you were to weigh him against his entire community he would outweigh them.'
2771.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dzar al-Ghifari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berkata: Ya Rasulullah, bagaimana kamu mengetahui bahwa kamu adalah seorang nabi hingga kamu yakin akan hal itu?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Wahai Abu Dzar, dua malaikat mendatangiku ketika aku berada di sebuah lembah di Makkah. Salah satunya turun ke bumi dan yang lainnya tetap di langit. Dikatakan: Apakah ini dia? Dikatakan: Ya. Dikatakan: Timbang dia dengan satu orang. Aku lebih berat darinya. Kemudian dikatakan: Timbang dia dengan seratus orang. Aku lebih berat dari mereka. Kemudian dikatakan: Timbang dia dengan seribu orang. Aku lebih berat dari mereka. Maka yang di langit berkata kepada yang di bumi: Bahkan jika kamu menimbangnya dengan seluruh umatnya pun dia tetap lebih berat dari mereka.'

2772.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Salih (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to call out to the people: 'O people, I am only a gifted mercy.'
2772.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Shalih (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa berseru kepada orang-orang: 'Wahai manusia, sesungguhnya aku hanyalah rahmat yang dihadiahkan.'

2773.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on a journey when a Bedouin approached. When he drew near, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: Where are you headed? He said: To my family. He said: Do you want something better than that? He said: What is that? He said: That you bear witness that there is no deity except Allah alone with no partner, that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger. He said: Who will bear witness to your truthfulness? He said: This tree. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ called to the tree while it was at the edge of the valley and it came and stood before him. He asked it to testify three times and it testified three times that he was as he claimed. Then it returned to its place.'
2773.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam perjalanan ketika seorang Arab Badui mendekat. Ketika ia mendekat, Rasulullah ﷺ berkata kepadanya: Hendak ke mana kamu? Ia berkata: Ke keluargaku. Beliau berkata: Apakah kamu menginginkan sesuatu yang lebih baik dari itu? Ia berkata: Apa itu? Beliau berkata: Bahwa kamu bersaksi tidak ada ilah selain Allah satu-satunya tanpa sekutu, dan Muhammad adalah hamba dan rasul-Nya. Ia berkata: Siapa yang akan bersaksi atas kebenaranmu? Beliau berkata: Pohon ini. Rasulullah ﷺ memanggil pohon tersebut yang berada di tepi lembah dan ia datang berdiri di hadapannya. Beliau memintanya bersaksi tiga kali dan ia bersaksi tiga kali bahwa beliau adalah sebagaimana yang dikatakannya. Kemudian ia kembali ke tempatnya.'

2774.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I went out with the Prophet ﷺ on a journey. He would not answer the call of nature until he was hidden from view. We descended near a valley and the Prophet ﷺ went to relieve himself. He saw two trees far apart, went to one of them and grabbed a branch, saying: Follow me with Allah's permission. It followed like a tame camel being led by its halter. He then did the same with the other tree until both were joined together. He relieved himself behind them, then they returned to their places.'
2774.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku pergi bersama Nabi ﷺ dalam perjalanan. Beliau tidak buang hajat sampai tersembunyi dari pandangan. Kami berhenti di dekat sebuah lembah dan Nabi ﷺ pergi untuk membuang hajat. Beliau melihat dua pohon yang berjauhan, pergi ke salah satunya dan memegang cabangnya, bersabda: Ikutilah aku dengan izin Allah. Ia mengikuti seperti unta jinak yang dituntun tali kekangnya. Kemudian beliau melakukan hal yang sama dengan pohon lainnya hingga keduanya bergabung bersama. Beliau membuang hajat di belakangnya, kemudian keduanya kembali ke tempat semula.'

2775.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We came with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ until we reached a garden belonging to the Banu al-Najjar, in which there was a camel that had become intractable and would not let anyone in. The Prophet ﷺ entered, called to it, and the camel came and knelt before him. He said: Bring me a rope. Then he handed it to its owner and said: Take care of your animals.'
2775.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami datang bersama Rasulullah ﷺ hingga tiba di sebuah kebun milik Banu al-Najjar, di dalamnya terdapat unta yang sulit dijinakkan dan tidak membiarkan siapapun masuk. Nabi ﷺ masuk, memanggilnya, dan unta itu datang berlutut di hadapannya. Beliau berkata: Bawakan aku tali. Kemudian beliau menyerahkannya kepada pemiliknya dan bersabda: Rawatlah hewan-hewan peliharaanmu.'

2776.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that a woman brought her son to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, my son is afflicted — he is struck by a fit every day, sometimes twice. He brought him close to him. The Prophet ﷺ blew into his mouth three times and said: In the name of Allah, I am the servant of Allah — get out, O enemy of Allah! Then he handed the boy back and said: You will never see what you feared from him again.'
2776.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa seorang wanita membawa anaknya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, anakku mengalami gangguan — ia diserang setiap hari, terkadang dua kali.' Beliau mendekatkan anak itu ke arahnya. Nabi ﷺ meniupkan ke mulutnya tiga kali dan bersabda: Bismillah, aku adalah hamba Allah — keluarlah, wahai musuh Allah! Kemudian beliau mengembalikan anak itu dan bersabda: Kamu tidak akan pernah melihat lagi apa yang kamu takutkan darinya.'

2777.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I know a stone in Makkah that used to greet me before my mission began. I still know it now.'
2777.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samurah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku mengenal sebuah batu di Makkah yang biasa memberiku salam sebelum aku diutus. Aku masih mengenalnya sekarang.'

2778.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him and his family) that he said: 'We were with the Prophet ﷺ in Makkah and went out with him to the outskirts. Whenever he passed by a tree or a rock, they would say: Peace be upon you, O Messenger of Allah.'
2778.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ali ibn Abi Thalib (radhiyallahu 'anhu wa alihi) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami bersama Nabi ﷺ di Makkah dan pergi bersamanya ke pinggiran kota. Setiap kali beliau melewati pohon atau batu, mereka berkata: Salam sejahtera atasmu, wahai Rasulullah.'

2779.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man from the tribe of Muzaynah or Juhaynah that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed Fajr and there were about a hundred wolves crouching in rows close to him. The wolves would not flee and the Prophet ﷺ was not troubled by them. He extended his hand and called one of them. It came to him and he stroked its head, then it returned to its place and the wolves departed.'
2779.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang pria dari suku Muzaynah atau Juhaynah bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mengerjakan shalat Subuh dan ada sekitar seratus serigala yang merunduk berjajar di dekatnya. Serigala-serigala itu tidak melarikan diri dan Nabi ﷺ tidak terganggu oleh mereka. Beliau mengulurkan tangannya dan memanggil salah satunya. Ia datang kepadanya dan beliau mengelus kepalanya, kemudian ia kembali ke tempatnya dan serigala-serigala itu pergi.'

2780.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Gabriel came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he was sitting sad, his face stained with blood — for the polytheists had wounded him. Gabriel said: What is the matter with you? He said: These people did this to me. Gabriel said: Would you like me to show you a sign? He said: Yes. Gabriel looked to a tree at the far end of the valley and said: Call it. He called it and it came until it stood before him. Gabriel said: Tell it to return. He told it and it returned to its place. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: That is enough for me.'
2780.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Jibril datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ ketika beliau sedang duduk dalam keadaan sedih, wajahnya berlumuran darah — karena orang-orang musyrik telah melukainya. Jibril berkata: Ada apa denganmu? Beliau berkata: Orang-orang ini melakukan ini kepadaku. Jibril berkata: Apakah kamu ingin aku menunjukkan sebuah tanda? Beliau berkata: Ya. Jibril melihat ke sebuah pohon di ujung lembah dan berkata: Panggilah ia. Beliau memanggilnya dan ia datang berdiri di hadapannya. Jibril berkata: Perintahkan ia untuk kembali. Beliau memerintahkannya dan ia kembali ke tempatnya. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Itu sudah cukup bagiku.'

2781.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that a man from the Banu Amir came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I will show you a sign.' He looked to a palm tree and said: Call that tree to come to you. He called it and it came dragging its roots until it stood before the Prophet ﷺ. Then the Prophet ﷺ said: Return to your place. It returned to its place. The man of Banu Amir said: O Banu Amir, I have never seen a greater magician than today!
2781.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa seorang pria dari Banu Amir datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku akan menunjukkan kepadamu sebuah tanda.' Beliau melihat ke sebuah pohon kurma dan bersabda: Panggilah pohon itu untuk datang kepadamu. Ia memanggilnya dan pohon itu datang menyeret akar-akarnya hingga berdiri di hadapan Nabi ﷺ. Kemudian Nabi ﷺ bersabda: Kembalilah ke tempatmu. Ia kembali ke tempatnya. Pria Banu Amir berkata: Wahai Banu Amir, aku tidak pernah melihat tukang sihir yang lebih hebat dari hari ini!

2782.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ called Bilal and asked for water, but Bilal went to search for water and came back with none. So the Prophet ﷺ placed his hand in a vessel and water began to flow from his fingers. The people performed ablution from it.'
2782.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ memanggil Bilal dan meminta air, namun Bilal pergi mencari air dan kembali tanpa membawa apa-apa. Maka Nabi ﷺ meletakkan tangannya ke dalam bejana dan air mulai mengalir dari jari-jarinya. Orang-orang berwudhu darinya.'

2783.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'We went out on a campaign or journey with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. At that time we were around three hundred men. We were thirsty, so the Prophet ﷺ called for a vessel in which there was barely enough water to wash someone's face. He placed his hand in it and water gushed forth from his fingers like springs. We drank and performed ablution.'
2783.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami pergi dalam sebuah ekspedisi atau perjalanan bersama Rasulullah ﷺ. Saat itu kami berjumlah sekitar tiga ratus orang. Kami kehausan, maka Nabi ﷺ minta diambilkan sebuah bejana yang hanya berisi air cukup untuk membasuh wajah seseorang. Beliau meletakkan tangannya di dalamnya dan air memancar dari jari-jarinya seperti mata air. Kami minum dan berwudhu darinya.'

2784.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'We were struck by thirst and rushed to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He placed his hand in a vessel and water flowed between his fingers. We drank from it and performed ablution, and we were one thousand and four hundred men.'
2784.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami ditimpa kehausan dan bergegas kepada Rasulullah ﷺ. Beliau meletakkan tangannya ke dalam bejana dan air mengalir di antara jari-jarinya. Kami minum darinya dan berwudhu, dan kami berjumlah seribu empat ratus orang.'

2785.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) through Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ complained of thirst. The Prophet ﷺ called for a vessel in which there was water and placed his hand in it. The water flowed from his fingers. The call was made for the people to come and perform ablution and drink until everyone had done so.'
2785.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) melalui Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Para sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ mengeluhkan kehausan. Nabi ﷺ minta diambilkan sebuah bejana yang berisi air dan meletakkan tangannya di dalamnya. Air mengalir dari jari-jarinya. Seruan dibuat agar orang-orang datang berwudhu dan minum hingga semua melakukannya.'

2786.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When we heard about the occurrence of a sinkhole, we recalled how we — the companions of Muhammad ﷺ — used to count miracles as blessings, whereas you count them as frightening. We used to count it a blessing that stones would glorify Allah while we were eating food with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
2786.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika kami mendengar tentang terjadinya tanah longsor, kami teringat bagaimana kami — para sahabat Muhammad ﷺ — biasa menganggap mukjizat sebagai berkah, sedangkan kalian menganggapnya menakutkan. Kami biasa menganggapnya sebagai berkah bahwa batu-batu bertasbih memuji Allah sementara kami sedang makan bersama Rasulullah ﷺ.'

2787.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The earth was shaken in the time of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud. He was told about it and said: O people, your Lord is giving you a warning — so heed His warning. Indeed it used to be that when the earth shook it was only because someone on it had committed a sin. However, I know of something that will not happen until near the end of time: the swallowing up of the east, the swallowing up of the west, and the swallowing up of the Arabian peninsula.'
2787.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Bumi berguncang pada zaman Abdullah ibn Mas'ud. Ia diberitahu tentang hal itu dan berkata: Wahai manusia, Tuhan kalian memberikan peringatan kepada kalian — maka perhatikanlah peringatan-Nya. Sesungguhnya dahulu ketika bumi berguncang hal itu hanya terjadi karena seseorang di atasnya melakukan dosa. Namun aku mengetahui sesuatu yang tidak akan terjadi sampai mendekati akhir zaman: penghisapan di timur, penghisapan di barat, dan penghisapan di Jazirah Arab.'

2788.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to deliver sermons leaning against a tree trunk. When a pulpit was made, the trunk groaned until the Prophet ﷺ came to it and placed his hand on it.
2788.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berkhutbah bersandar pada batang pohon. Ketika mimbar dibuat, batang pohon itu merintih hingga Nabi ﷺ mendatanginya dan meletakkan tangannya di atasnya.

2789.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraydah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When the Prophet ﷺ used to deliver a sermon, he would stand for a long time, and the standing was hard on him. So a man from the Ansar said: O Messenger of Allah, shall we make you a pulpit from which you may deliver sermons? He agreed and they made him a pulpit of two steps. On the day the pulpit was set, when the Prophet ﷺ stood on it, the tree trunk cried out like an infant until the Prophet ﷺ descended and embraced it, and it fell silent.'
2789.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraydah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Nabi ﷺ biasa berkhutbah, beliau berdiri dalam waktu yang lama, dan berdiri itu berat baginya. Maka seorang pria dari Anshar berkata: Ya Rasulullah, apakah kami boleh membuat mimbar untukmu agar kamu dapat berkhutbah darinya? Beliau setuju dan mereka membuat mimbar dua anak tangga. Pada hari mimbar dipasang, ketika Nabi ﷺ berdiri di atasnya, batang pohon menangis seperti bayi hingga Nabi ﷺ turun dan memeluknya, lalu batang itu diam.'

2790.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to stand by a tree trunk before the pulpit was built. When the pulpit was built and he stood on it, the trunk cried out until the Prophet ﷺ came to it and embraced it, and it fell silent.'
2790.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berdiri di sisi batang pohon sebelum mimbar dibangun. Ketika mimbar dibangun dan beliau berdiri di atasnya, batang itu menangis hingga Nabi ﷺ mendatanginya dan memeluknya, lalu batang itu diam.'

2791.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ used to deliver sermons from a piece of wood. When the pulpit was made, he stood on it and the wood cried out like a young camel until the Prophet ﷺ descended and placed his hand on it.'
2791.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ biasa berkhutbah dari sepotong kayu. Ketika mimbar dibuat, beliau berdiri di atasnya dan kayu itu menangis seperti unta muda hingga Nabi ﷺ turun dan meletakkan tangannya di atasnya.'

2792.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The wood cried out like the groaning of a she-camel who has been separated from her young.'
2792.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kayu itu menangis seperti rintihan unta betina yang dipisahkan dari anaknya.'

2793.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubayy ibn Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to pray and deliver sermons toward a tree trunk. When the pulpit was built he used it instead. The tree trunk cried out and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came down from the pulpit and embraced it. Then he was given the choice between being replanted in the garden where it had come from — where it would grow and give fruit until the Day of Judgment — or being buried so that it would be resurrected with righteous people. He chose to be buried.'
2793.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubay ibn Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa shalat dan berkhutbah menghadap batang pohon. Ketika mimbar dibangun beliau menggunakannya sebagai gantinya. Batang pohon itu menangis dan Rasulullah ﷺ turun dari mimbar dan memeluknya. Kemudian ia diberi pilihan antara ditanam kembali di kebun asalnya — di mana ia akan tumbuh dan berbuah hingga Hari Kiamat — atau dikubur sehingga ia akan dibangkitkan bersama orang-orang saleh. Ia memilih untuk dikubur.'

2794.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to deliver sermons leaning against a tree trunk. A Roman man came to him and said: Shall I make you something on which you can sit and it will look as if you are standing? He made him a pulpit with two steps and a seat. On the first Friday the Prophet ﷺ sat on it and the trunk cried out like a pregnant she-camel calling for her calf. The Prophet ﷺ descended from it and embraced it until it fell silent.'
2794.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berkhutbah bersandar pada batang pohon. Seorang pria Romawi datang kepadanya dan berkata: Bolehkah aku membuat sesuatu untukmu yang bisa kamu duduki dan tampak seolah-olah kamu berdiri? Ia membuat mimbar dengan dua anak tangga dan tempat duduk. Pada Jumat pertama Nabi ﷺ duduk di atasnya dan batang pohon menangis seperti unta betina hamil yang memanggil anaknya. Nabi ﷺ turun darinya dan memeluknya hingga diam.'

2795.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Hasan al-Basri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When the Prophet ﷺ came to Madinah, he used to lean his back against a piece of wood and address the people. When they multiplied around him, he desired a pulpit. A man from the Ansar said: Shall I make you a pulpit? He made it, and when the pulpit was set up and the Prophet ﷺ ascended it, the wood cried out with a sound like thunder until the Masjid shook. He ﷺ descended and embraced it until it fell silent. He said: By Him in Whose hand is my soul, had I not embraced it, it would have cried until the Day of Resurrection out of longing for the remembrance of Allah.'
2795.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Hasan al-Bashri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Nabi ﷺ datang ke Madinah, beliau biasa menyandarkan punggungnya pada sepotong kayu dan berbicara kepada orang-orang. Ketika mereka bertambah banyak di sekelilingnya, beliau menginginkan mimbar. Seorang pria dari Anshar berkata: Bolehkah aku membuat mimbar untukmu? Ia membuatnya, dan ketika mimbar dipasang dan Nabi ﷺ naik ke atasnya, kayu itu menangis dengan suara seperti guntur hingga masjid bergetar. Beliau ﷺ turun dan memeluknya hingga diam. Beliau bersabda: Demi Dzat yang jiwaku ada dalam tangan-Nya, seandainya aku tidak memeluknya, ia akan terus menangis hingga Hari Kiamat karena kerinduan terhadap dzikrullah.'

2796.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that the Prophet ﷺ used to deliver sermons leaning against a tree trunk before the pulpit was made. When the pulpit was made, the tree trunk cried out with a voice that the whole mosque heard until the Prophet ﷺ descended from the pulpit and embraced it and it fell silent.
2796.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa berkhutbah bersandar pada batang pohon sebelum mimbar dibuat. Ketika mimbar dibuat, batang pohon itu menangis dengan suara yang seluruh masjid mendengarnya hingga Nabi ﷺ turun dari mimbar dan memeluknya lalu diam.

2797.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sahl ibn Sa'd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The piece of wood from which the Prophet ﷺ used to stand when delivering sermons cried out. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went to it and placed his hand on it.'
2797.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sahl ibn Sa'd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Sepotong kayu yang biasa digunakan Nabi ﷺ berdiri ketika berkhutbah menangis. Rasulullah ﷺ mendatanginya dan meletakkan tangannya di atasnya.'
